       c.code AS course_code,

       COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS enrolled_students

  ON cs.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN public.enrollments AS e
  ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  AND e.is_active = true
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT c.id AS course_id,
       c.code AS course_code,
       c.name AS course_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS enrolled_students
FROM public.courses AS c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections AS cs
  ON cs.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN public.enrollments AS e
  ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  AND e.is_active = true
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY enrolled_students DESC, c.code ASC;

issues: The SQL uses LEFT JOINs so courses with zero active enrollments will appear with enrolled_students = 0. If you intended to exclude courses without any active enrollments, use INNER JOIN (or filter WHERE enrolled_students > 0).
A bar chart with course_name on the x-axis can get crowded for many courses (rowCount=26). Consider a horizontal bar chart, paging, or interactive filtering if labels overlap despite labelAngle=-40.

result.sql:
SELECT c.id AS course_id,
       c.code AS course_code,
       c.name AS course_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS enrolled_students
FROM public.courses AS c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections AS cs
  ON cs.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN public.enrollments AS e
  ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  AND e.is_active = true
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY enrolled_students DESC, c.code ASC;

issues: The SQL uses LEFT JOINs so courses with zero active enrollments will appear with enrolled_students = 0. If you intended to exclude courses without any active enrollments, use INNER JOIN (or filter WHERE enrolled_students > 0).
A bar chart with course_name on the x-axis can get crowded for many courses (rowCount=26). Consider a horizontal bar chart, paging, or interactive filtering if labels overlap despite labelAngle=-40.
